---
title: "Research"
---

My research uses molecular simulation to understand interfacial phenomena that govern ice formation, heat flow, and phase change at the nanoscale. I lead a group at the **University of Edinburgh** funded by an **ERC Starting Grant** ([NANO-COOL](https://gtr.ukri.org/projects?ref=EP%2FY036107%2F1), 2024-2029). Our work falls into three themes, mapped on the phase diagram below. Click a marker to explore.

**Ice Nucleation and Surface Engineering.** We study how ice forms on [nanostructured surfaces](https://doi.org/10.1063/5.0069896), how [surface vibrations influence ice nucleation](https://doi.org/10.1039/D5NR00326A), and the design principles behind [icephobic coatings](https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-030-82992-6_8).

**Interfacial Heat Transfer.** Thermal transport across solid-liquid interfaces depends on atomic-scale structure. We investigate [spectral mechanisms](https://doi.org/10.1039/D4CP04768K) of interfacial thermal conductance and how [surface chemistry governs conductance regimes](https://doi.org/10.1021/acs.jpcc.4c00536).

**Phase Change and Bubble Dynamics.** We study [heterogeneous bubble nucleation](https://doi.org/10.1063/5.0259208) in superheated liquids, how [wettability shapes bubble growth](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ijheatmasstransfer.2023.124657), [inertio-thermal growth dynamics](https://doi.org/10.1017/jfm.2022.734) from molecular to continuum scales, [acoustothermal nanobubble nucleation](https://doi.org/10.1021/acs.nanolett.0c03895), and [acoustothermal atomization](https://doi.org/10.1103/PhysRevLett.121.104502) of water nanofilms.

<div id="research-diagram-wrap">
<div id="research-diagram"></div>
<div id="research-tooltip" class="research-tooltip"><div class="rt-title"></div><div class="rt-desc"></div></div>
<div id="research-panel" class="research-panel"><button class="rp-close">&times;</button><h3 class="rp-title"></h3><p class="rp-narrative"></p><div class="rp-papers"></div></div>
</div>

---

## Methods

<div id="methods-pipeline-wrap">
<div id="methods-pipeline"></div>
<div id="methods-tooltip" class="methods-tooltip"><div class="mt-title"></div><div class="mt-desc"></div></div>
</div>

---

## Group Website

For more details on our research activities, visit the [mfX Group Website](https://multiscaleflowx.github.io/).
